primary_accounting_issue: Eligibility of foreign exchange risk hedge accounting for intragroup dividend receivables in consolidated financial statements under IFRS

primary_authority.reason: IFRS 9 defines eligible hedged items for hedge accounting, including recognized assets, and paragraph 6.3.5 restricts hedged items to parties external to the reporting entity while providing an exception for intragroup monetary items with FX exposure affecting consolidated profit or loss

IAS 21 requires recognition of foreign exchange differences on intragroup monetary items in consolidated financial statements when entities have different functional currencies, creating the underlying FX exposure

supporting_authority.reason: IFRIC 16 clarifies that hedge accounting for net investments applies only when net assets are included in financial statements and specifies the conditions under which intragroup FX exposure qualifies for hedge accounting

peripheral_authority.reason: IAS 24 addresses related party disclosure requirements but does not govern hedge accounting eligibility for intragroup transactions
IFRS 12 addresses disclosure requirements for interests in subsidiaries, joint arrangements, and associates but does not address hedge accounting treatment

treatment_families.family: Hedge accounting for foreign exchange risk on intragroup monetary items

approaches.label: Hedge accounting eligible for intragroup FX exposure
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: When intragroup dividend receivables involve entities with different functional currencies, the resulting foreign exchange exposure is recognized in consolidated profit or loss under IAS 21 paragraph 45, and IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.5 permits such intragroup monetary items to qualify as hedged items

approaches.label: Hedge accounting ineligible for same-functional-currency intragroup items
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: When intragroup dividend receivables are between entities with the same functional currency, no foreign exchange exposure exists in the consolidated financial statements, and therefore hedge accounting for FX risk is not applicable
